summary: The feedback system is file-based end to end: there is NO Prisma Feedback table. All three working paths (tRPC feedback router, Electron IPC, and the diagnostic MCP tools via scripts/analysis/feedback-utils.js) converge on the single file context/feedback.json (~170 items), so in dev the agent's MCP tools DO see client-submitted feedback. Desktop and web clients can submit via a shared DevTools panel (Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+D -> FeedbackForm -> feedback-service with a tRPC -> IPC -> localStorage fallback chain). iOS and Vision Pro have no feedback capability at all (greps only hit haptic/animation 'feedback'). The requirement's 'central table' is unmet (it is a JSON file with three unsynchronized writer processes and cwd-dependent paths), the web client silently strands feedback in localStorage when the API key is missing, and the legacy IPC path still bypasses the server.

clientPaths.detail: DevTools modal opens via Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+D (src/renderer/App.tsx:308-312, mounted :1022-1024) -> FeedbackForm tab (src/renderer/components/dev/DevTools.tsx:9,25) -> saveFeedback from src/renderer/services/feedback-service.ts (FeedbackForm.tsx:6,94). Transport fallback chain (feedback-service.ts:124-164): (1) tRPC feedback.save via createDynamicClient using window.appConfig from preload (src/preload/index.ts:16-27, serverUrl/apiKey from TASK_PLANNER_* env); (2) Electron IPC window.electronAPI.saveFeedback -> 'feedback:save' (src/preload/index.ts:42; handler src/main/index.ts:54-123 writes process.cwd()/context/feedback.json); (3) localStorage 'task-planner-feedback'. Both real paths land in context/feedback.json in dev. FeedbackViewer reads/resolves via the same service (FeedbackViewer.tsx:57,75).

src/web/main.tsx mounts the same App (no Electron deps); window.appConfig injected in src/web/index.html:29-34 (serverUrl=window.location.origin, apiKey from localStorage 'taskPlannerApiKey', settable via ?apiKey= URL param, :15-27). Server serves the web build from dist/web (src/server/index.ts:105-126). Same DevTools shortcut -> FeedbackForm -> feedback-service: hasTrpcAccess() true (feedback-service.ts:51-57) -> trpc feedback.save -> server writes context/feedback.json. CAVEAT (works-with-a-trap): protectedProcedure requires a valid x-api-key (src/server/trpc.ts:32-35,95); with no/invalid key the tRPC call throws, there is no electronAPI, and saveFeedback silently falls through to localStorage and returns true (feedback-service.ts:147-163) — feedback is stranded on the device while the UI reports success; nothing ever syncs it. Also sessionId falls back to 'unknown' (FeedbackForm.tsx:79, no electronAPI.getSessionId on web).

No feedback submission feature exists. grep -ril 'feedback' over TaskPlannerMobile/TaskPlannerMobile/**/*.swift only matches UIImpactFeedbackGenerator/UINotificationFeedbackGenerator haptics (e.g. Features/Chat/ChatViewModel.swift:118,160; Features/Now/NowViewModel.swift:143-144,238; Features/Board/BoardViewModel.swift:135,154). No FeedbackService, no tRPC wrapper for feedback.*, no UI.

No feedback submission feature. The only 'feedback' matches are animation doc comments: Spatial/Systems/PulseSystem.swift:4, Spatial/Components/PulseComponent.swift:4, Spatial/DesignSystem/SpatialMotion.swift:26 ('one-shot scale feedback'). Neither SpatialRoot services nor ManagementWindow expose any feedback path.

mcp__diagnostic__add_feedback / get_next_feedback / resolve_feedback (scripts/mcp/diagnostic-wrapper.ts:51,70,84; dispatch :332-339) shell out to node scripts/analysis/feedback-utils.js (:394-446; compiled dist/diagnostic-wrapper.js:467-522), which reads/writes context/feedback.json directly (FEEDBACK_FILE at feedback-utils.js:21, writes :199,:229). add stamps sessionId 'cli-feedback-utils' (:206-229); resolve is case-insensitive title substring match (:180-201).

centralStore: There is exactly ONE intended central store, but it is a FILE, not a table: context/feedback.json (~170 items, array of {type, priority, title, description, components?, steps?, expected?, actual?, timestamp, sessionId, resolved?, resolvedDate?, resolvedIn?}). Evidence of NO DB table: prisma/schema.prisma contains no Feedback model (full model list grepped — ContextEntry..TaskComparison, none feedback-related), and src/server/router/feedback.ts:4-7 explicitly documents 'Feedback is stored in context/feedback.json (file-based, not in database)'. Note: CLAUDE.md's 'check feedback.json' refers to this context/ file; there is no repo-root feedback.json. Three independent writer processes target the same file via different path resolution: (1) tRPC router: path.join(process.cwd(), 'context', 'feedback.json') (feedback.ts:53-55); (2) Electron main IPC: path.join(process.cwd(), 'context', 'feedback.json') (src/main/index.ts:56-57,129-130,141-143,153); (3) feedback-utils.js (MCP backend): path.join(__dirname, '..', '..', 'context', 'feedback.json') (feedback-utils.js:21 — cwd-independent). In dev they coincide (server runs via 'dotenv ... tsx src/server/index.ts' from repo root, package.json:21). Divergence risks: (a) any server/Electron launch with cwd != repo root (packaged app) writes a DIFFERENT file than MCP reads; (b) a FOURTH shadow store exists — browser localStorage 'task-planner-feedback' (feedback-service.ts:15), the silent last-resort sink with no sync-back; (c) all writers do non-atomic read-modify-write JSON rewrites with no locking, so concurrent server/Electron/MCP writes lose updates, and feedback.update / 'feedback:update' replace the ENTIRE array (feedback.ts:137-152; main/index.ts:150-186), so a FeedbackViewer resolve can clobber an item the MCP added in between.
mcpIntegration: The diagnostic MCP server is scripts/mcp/diagnostic-wrapper.ts, run compiled from scripts/mcp/dist/diagnostic-wrapper.js per .mcp.json. Its three feedback tools do NOT touch the server or the DB — each spawns 'node scripts/analysis/feedback-utils.js' (script path resolved via process.cwd(): diagnostic-wrapper.ts:396,417,436): get_next_feedback -> callFeedbackUtils (:332-333,:394-413, commands unresolved/summary/high/by-type), resolve_feedback -> callResolveFeedback (:335-336,:415-429, 'resolve <title-substring>'), add_feedback -> callAddFeedback (:338-346,:431-446, 'add <type> <priority> <title> <description>'). feedback-utils.js reads and writes context/feedback.json directly via fs (FEEDBACK_FILE feedback-utils.js:21; writeFileSync :199 resolve, :229 add). CONCLUSION: MCP tools and the tRPC feedback router DO share the same store today (the JSON file), so feedback submitted from desktop/web is visible to the agent — the chain is not split between file and DB, it is simply all-file. The integration is fragile though: it depends on the MCP process and the tRPC server both having repo root as cwd, it bypasses the router's Zod validation and dedupe (feedback-utils.js add does no timestamp+sessionId dedupe), and it races the server's read-modify-write.
gaps: No central TABLE: requirement says feedback routes to a central table, but no Prisma Feedback model exists; the store is context/feedback.json (src/server/router/feedback.ts:4-7,53-55; prisma/schema.prisma has no Feedback model).
iOS client has zero feedback capability — no UI, no service, no feedback.* tRPC wrapper (only haptic-generator matches in TaskPlannerMobile/TaskPlannerMobile Swift).
Vision Pro client has zero feedback capability — only animation doc-comment matches (PulseSystem.swift:4, PulseComponent.swift:4, SpatialMotion.swift:26).

Web client silent data stranding: with a missing/invalid API key, protectedProcedure rejects (src/server/trpc.ts:32-35,95) and saveFeedback silently writes localStorage and returns true (feedback-service.ts:147-163) — user sees success, feedback never reaches the central store, and nothing ever flushes localStorage back.

Legacy Electron IPC path still live and bypasses the server: preload feedback:save/read/load/update (src/preload/index.ts:41-46) -> src/main/index.ts:54-186 writes context/feedback.json from a second process; combined with the server process this is unsynchronized read-modify-write on one JSON file (lost-update races).

Full-array replace semantics: feedback.update (feedback.ts:137-152) and 'feedback:update' (main/index.ts:150-186) overwrite the whole file — last writer wins; a resolve from FeedbackViewer can erase items concurrently added via MCP or another client.

cwd-dependent store paths: router and Electron main use process.cwd()/context/feedback.json; a packaged app or server launched from another directory writes a different file than the MCP tools read (feedback-utils.js:21 uses __dirname and always hits the repo copy).

No stable item identity: dedupe key is timestamp+sessionId (feedback.ts:97-100), MCP resolve matches by title substring (feedback-utils.js:180-201), and web submissions all share sessionId 'unknown' (FeedbackForm.tsx:79) — collisions and wrong-item resolution are possible.
Adjacent finding — MCP add_feedback bypasses the router's Zod schema and dedupe entirely (writes raw object, no timestamp+sessionId duplicate check, feedback-utils.js:206-229).
Adjacent finding — feedback discoverability: the only entry point on desktop/web is the undocumented Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+D DevTools shortcut (App.tsx:308-312); there is no visible feedback button.

Adjacent finding — diagnostic-wrapper.ts locates feedback-utils.js via process.cwd() (:396,417,436); if an MCP host ever launches it with a different cwd the feedback tools fail even though .mcp.json uses absolute paths for the wrapper itself.
fixPlan: Minimal change set to satisfy 'every client -> tRPC feedback router -> one Prisma table -> MCP reads the same table': (1) SCHEMA: add a Feedback model to prisma/schema.prisma (id String @id @default(cuid()), type String, priority String, title String, description String, components String? (JSON), steps/expected/actual String?, sessionId String, createdAt DateTime @default(now()), resolved Boolean @default(false), resolvedDate DateTime?, resolvedIn String?); migrate via mcp__database__safe_migrate --name add_feedback_table (never db push). (2) SERVER: rewrite src/server/router/feedback.ts to Prisma-backed procedures keeping protectedProcedure (feedback is intentionally session-independent): list (optionally filtered by resolved/type/priority), add (returns id; keep a timestamp+sessionId uniqueness pre-check for legacy clients), resolve({id}), update({id, patch}) — replacing the dangerous full-array 'update'. Add a one-time import script that loads existing context/feedback.json rows into the table (run once, keep the file as read-only archive), plus Vitest coverage. (3) MCP: repoint the tools at the same table by rewriting scripts/analysis/feedback-utils.js (or a TS replacement) to use @prisma/client with the same DATABASE_URL instead of fs — get_next_feedback orders unresolved by priority/createdAt, add_feedback inserts, resolve_feedback resolves by id (keep title-substring as a convenience that resolves via id lookup); diagnostic-wrapper.ts needs no tool-surface change. (4) DESKTOP: delete the IPC feedback handlers (src/main/index.ts:50-186) and preload methods (src/preload/index.ts:41-46); in src/renderer/services/feedback-service.ts drop the IPC branch, make tRPC the sole transport, and convert localStorage from a silent sink into an explicit offline QUEUE: on failure return {queued:true} so the UI shows 'saved locally, will retry', and flush the queue to feedback.add on next successful connection/app start. (5) iOS + VISION PRO: add a thin FeedbackService Swift wrapper over the shared TRPCClient calling feedback.add (x-api-key only, no x-session-id needed); UI = a small form sheet — iOS: a 'Send Feedback' row in settings; Vision Pro: a button in ManagementWindow (file must live under TaskPlannerVision/ for target membership, or be enrolled in Core/ via pbxproj per the target-membership rule); verify with xcodebuild, not SourceKit. (6) DOCS: update CLAUDE.md / .claude/rules to say feedback lives in the Feedback table and is read via mcp__diagnostic__get_next_feedback (retire 'check feedback.json'). This collapses four stores (file, DB-none, localStorage, IPC-file) into one table with one transport and removes all cwd/race hazards.
